chapter_title.ar: باب حَدِّ الْمَسِيرِ الَّذِي تُقْصَرُ فِيهِ الصَّلَاةُ
chapter_title.en: Chapter 77 – A limit of the traveller at which he would shorten the Salāt

gradings: Allamah Baqir al-Majlisi: حسن - Mir‘at al ‘Uqul Fi Sharh Akhbar Al al Rasul (15/375)
Allamah Baqir al-Majlisi: حسن - Mir‘at al ‘Uqul Fi Sharh Akhbar Al al Rasul (15/375)
text: قُلْتُ لِأَبِي عَبْدِ اللَّهِ ( عليه السلام ) أَدْنَى مَا يَقْصُرُ فِيهِ الْمُسَافِرُ فَقَالَ بَرِيدٌ .

en.hubeali: And from him, from his father, from Ibn Abu Umeyr, from Abu Ayoub who said,
‘I said to Abu Abdullahasws, ‘The least of what the traveler would be shortening (his Salāt) in?’ So heasws said: ‘A Bareyd (12 miles approx.)’.
en.sarwar: 2. It is a narration from him (narrator of previous Hadith) by his father from ibn abu ‘Umayr from abu Ayyub who has said the following: “I once asked abu ‘Abd Allah, ‘Alayhi al-Salam, about the minimum distance after the traveling of which a traveler reduces his Salah (prayer). He (the Imam) said, ‘It is one barid.’”
